title: Time Horizon Portrait

introduction.subtitle: How do you relate to past, present, and future? Five time orientations, none of them the "right" one.
introduction.description:
This is not about balance versus imbalance — different time orientations serve different life phases. It shows which orientations currently dominate you, so you can tell whether your time horizon is chosen or just inherited.

Inspired by Zimbardo Time Perspective concepts. Items are original (ZTPI is copyrighted and not reproduced).

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.duration: 15 statements. Approximately three to four minutes.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: Inspired by Zimbardo Time Perspective concepts. Items are original. This is not the validated ZTPI and not a clinical assessment.

questions.text: I enjoy looking back on good memories from my past.

conceptHelp.title: Past-Positive: I enjoy looking back on good memories from my past.
conceptHelp.summary: Past-Positive means a warm, stabilizing relationship with good memories, roots, and continuity from the past. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "I enjoy looking back on good memories from my past.".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Self-knowledge items are useful when they help you notice repeated behavior. They become misleading if one answer is treated as a permanent identity or diagnosis.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think across normal weeks, not one unusually good or bad day. Rate the statement by how often it describes your real preference or behavior.
conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

questions.text: I feel warmly about the traditions I grew up with.

conceptHelp.title: Past-Positive: I feel warmly about the traditions I grew up with.
conceptHelp.summary: Past-Positive means a warm, stabilizing relationship with good memories, roots, and continuity from the past. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "I feel warmly about the traditions I grew up with.".

questions.text: Thinking about my past gives me a sense of stability and gratitude.

conceptHelp.title: Past-Positive: Thinking about my past gives me a sense of stability and gratitude.
conceptHelp.summary: Past-Positive means a warm, stabilizing relationship with good memories, roots, and continuity from the past. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "Thinking about my past gives me a sense of stability and gratitude.".

questions.text: I often replay mistakes from my past and wish I had acted differently.

conceptHelp.title: Past-negative: I often replay mistakes from my past and wish I had acted differently.
conceptHelp.summary: Past-negative means a tendency for regret, unresolved pain, or past mistakes to pull attention back. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "I often replay mistakes from my past and wish I had acted differently.".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Self-knowledge items are useful when they help you notice repeated behavior. They become misleading if one answer is treated as a permanent identity or diagnosis.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think across normal weeks, not one unusually good or bad day. Rate the statement by how often it describes your real preference or behavior.
conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

questions.text: I follow my impulses and do what feels good in the moment.

conceptHelp.title: Present-hedonistic: I follow my impulses and do what feels good in the moment.
conceptHelp.summary: Present-hedonistic means orientation toward enjoyment, stimulation, spontaneity, and present experience. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "I follow my impulses and do what feels good in the moment.".

questions.text: It feels like much of what happens to me is out of my hands.

conceptHelp.title: Present-fatalistic: It feels like much of what happens to me is out of my hands.
conceptHelp.summary: Present-fatalistic means the sense that planning or effort has limited effect because events feel outside personal control. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "It feels like much of what happens to me is out of my hands.".

attribution.text: Inspired by Zimbardo Time Perspective concepts. Items are original and not from the validated ZTPI instrument.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
